channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Mixed items left on the pavement, 12th June
item.description:
Could you please remove all the items that have been left on the pavement, including the orange barrier? Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): King John Court, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Exposed open jagged glass on walk way, 12th June
item.description:
Exposed open jagged glass on walk way for four days. Hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Trecastle Way, Tufnell Park, Greater London

item.title: Dumped fridge and large mirror, 12th June

item.title: Tarmac broken away outside 9 warren road, 12th June
item.description:
There is some tarmac outside number 9 warren road that is braking down, this is causing issues with the surface being uneven. The resident is a wheelchair user and this is very important that it gets sorted. Old ref number 9290721- this was marked as fixed this morning and it isnt.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warren Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parking signs blacked out again, 12th June
item.description:
This is a recurring problem, same individual spaying out signs as he pleases, more frequently than Southwark Council are able to clean them 🤦‍♂️
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fielding Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Corner of Claremont road, 12th June
item.description:
I reported this dumped chair on Thursday - still not removed please remove

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Claremont Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Dumped car, 12th June
item.description:
Car has been here for 4 months
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wadeville Close, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Incorrectly designated “bee area” – safety, access and nuisance problem, 12th June
item.description:
The grass verge outside the bungalows 51-65 on Cameron Road has been left uncut and I was informed it is a “designated bee area”. However: • There is no statutory signage, unlike other designated verges in the area • Residents received no consultation or notification, despite the verge directly affecting access to our homes • The overgrowth is now obstructing visibility, making it difficult for delivery drivers and potentially emergency services to locate our OFF ROAD properties • Weeds from the verge are blowing into our gardens, causing property nuisance and undermining paid‑for weed‑control treatments • Dumped items AND DOGS MESS BAGS are accumulating in the long grass This appears to be a mistaken or inappropriate designation, and the verge does not meet the criteria for a biodiversity zone due to its location, usage, and the need for clear sightlines. Please review this verge urgently and reinstate scheduled mowing.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 67, Cameron Road, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Marlings Park Ave road and pavements, 12th June
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 12/06/26 12:57 I would like to enquire what plane the council have to repair the poor state of pavements and driveways in our road. The surfaces are cracked and uneven and a serious trip hazard to the elderly and disabled. I myself have already fallen once but was fortunate to land with just a few cuts and bruises to show for the fall. Overall, the poor state of both the roads and in particular the pavements is a growing cause for concern . Please kindly advise if the council has a scheme of works in place to rectify the currently poor state of the surfaces. Kind regards
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Marlings Park Avenue, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Winkworth Boards on park railings, 12th June
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sutton Place, Hackney, London

item.title: More flytipping, 12th June

item.title: TV dumped opposite15 Orient street, 12th June
item.description:
I reported this at09.09 TODAY, I had an email at 11.09 saying it had been cleared. IT is now 14.30 and the tv is STILL OPPOSITE 15 Orient street as I write this. Please sort this problem as soon as possible

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Orient Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
